content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN and an array containing NNN integers.

For each element in the array, you have to output its frequency in the array using Hashing.

The frequency of an element in the array tells how many times it occurs in the array.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN denoting the number of elements in the array.
The next line contains NNN space separated integers, denoting the elements in the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output NNN space separated integers denoting the frequency of each element of the array.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the size of the array. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1, A2, …, AN.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output N space‑separated integers on a single line. The i‑th integer is the frequency of Ai in the whole array of that test case.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: frequencies are 1→4, 2→3, 3→2, 4→1. Each element is replaced by its count. Test case 2: all five numbers are 1, frequency 5. Test case 3: 1 appears 3 times, 2 appears 2 times.
Only one element, its frequency is 1.
Number 5 occurs three times, 6 occurs once.

solution.approach: Count the occurrences of each possible value (1…100) using a fixed size frequency array. First pass over the input array increments freq[value]. In a second pass, output freq[value] for each element in the original order. The frequency array is re‑initialised for every test case, giving O(N) time and O(101) extra space per case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) for storing the input array plus O(101) for the frequency table (both negligible compared to N).

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.buffer.read().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        N = int(next(it))
        arr = [int(next(it)) for _ in range(N)]
        freq = [0] * 101
        for v in arr:
            freq[v] += 1
        out_lines.append(' '.join(str(freq[v]) for v in arr))
    sys.stdout.write('
'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
